prerequisites: A Plausible Analytics account with at least one site added (either on plausible.io cloud or a self-hosted instance).
The site_id, which is the domain of the site as it appears in your Plausible account (for example example.com).
A Stats API key generated from your Plausible account settings.
For a self-hosted Plausible instance, the base URL of that instance to set as api_url (the cloud default is https://plausible.io).

authMethods.name: API key (Bearer token)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with the api_key secret field. Plausible's Stats API requires each request to carry the key in the Authorization header using the Bearer token method (Authorization: Bearer YOUR-KEY). Generate the key under your account settings in the API Keys section.

setupSteps.title: Create a Stats API key
setupSteps.body: Log in to your Plausible account. Open the menu under your account name in the top right, go to settings, then select API Keys in the left sidebar. Create a new key and copy it, then provide it as the api_key field. API keys have a default rate limit of 600 requests per hour.

setupSteps.title: Identify your site_id
setupSteps.body: Set the required site_id field to the domain of the site you want to query, entered exactly as it appears in your Plausible dashboard (for example example.com). The Stats API uses this value to scope queries to that site.

setupSteps.title: Set the API URL (self-hosted only)
setupSteps.body: Leave api_url empty to query Plausible Cloud at the default host (https://plausible.io). If you run a self-hosted Plausible instance, set api_url to your instance's base URL so requests are sent to your own server instead of the cloud.

setupSteps.title: Set an optional start date
setupSteps.body: Optionally provide start_date to bound the historical window the sync begins from. If omitted, the connector uses its default range.

sources.title: Plausible Stats API v1 reference (authentication via Bearer token)
Get started with the Stats API using Postman (obtaining an API key)
